bridge_interface_contract.input_contract: A future bridge may expose CMS records as read-only documents with provenance, timestamp, source path, confidence/validation tier, and claim boundary.
bridge_interface_contract.output_contract: Hermes may produce proposals, classifications, and dry-run plans only; no apply/write action is authorized by this design.

bridge_purpose: Design a future path for Hermes to read CMS context as orientation/evidence without granting CMS write authority or runtime command authority.
forbidden_v0_4_behaviors: load live private memory as authority
non_claim_lock: HRCN v0.4 is a docs/context bridge-design layer. It defines a future CMS read-only bridge boundary. It does not create a loader, adapter, runtime bridge, CMS folder, memory writer, repair applier, API writer, or live integration. It does not modify Hermes runtime behavior, prompts, tools, skills, plugins, providers, gateways, TUI, web runtime, package dependencies, memory authority, API write authority, CMS write authority, security posture, production readiness, autonomy, consciousness, sentience, AGI/ASI status, or external validation.
primary_law: A CMS bridge may inform Hermes; it may not command Hermes.
